assignments.title: Introduction to Discrete Mathematics Quiz
assignments.description: The Introduction to Discrete Mathematics Quiz assesses your understanding of the fundamental concepts of discrete mathematics, including set theory, relations, functions, and mathematical proofs. This quiz will cover the basic definitions, properties, and operations of sets, as well as introductory concepts in relations and functions. You will be required to identify key terms, apply definitions, and solve basic problems related to these topics.
requirements: Read chapters 1-2 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen.
Review the lecture notes and class discussions on introductory discrete mathematics concepts.
Complete the practice problems provided in the textbook and class materials.
Take the quiz during the designated class period, following the instructions provided.
Ensure all work is your own and properly cited if required.

assignments.title: Basic Set Theory Exercises
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will complete a series of exercises to demonstrate their understanding of basic set theory concepts. Using the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen, students will work through selected problems that cover set operations, Venn diagrams, and basic set properties. The goal is to apply theoretical knowledge to practical problems, ensuring a solid grasp of foundational concepts.
requirements: Read Chapter 1 of the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen, focusing on set theory.
Complete problems 1-10 on page 23 of the textbook, which cover basic set operations and properties.
Draw Venn diagrams for problems 3, 5, and 7 to visualize set relationships.
Write out solutions to problems 1, 4, and 9, showing all steps and explanations.

assignments.title: Mathematical Proofs Checklist
assignments.description: Complete the mathematical proofs checklist by working through each problem and providing a step-by-step proof for each. Ensure that your proofs are clear, concise, and easy to follow. The checklist is designed to help you understand and apply the principles of mathematical proofs, which are a fundamental concept in discrete mathematics. Refer to the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen for guidance on mathematical proofs.
requirements: Work through each problem on the checklist and provide a complete proof
Ensure that each proof is step-by-step and easy to follow
Use proper mathematical notation and terminology throughout the proofs
Review the textbook for guidance on mathematical proofs and apply the concepts to the problems on the checklist

assignments.title: Relations and Functions Quiz
assignments.description: For this quiz, students will demonstrate their understanding of relations and functions in discrete mathematics. The quiz will cover the definition and properties of relations and functions, including reflexive, symmetric, and transitive properties, as well as one-to-one and onto functions. Students will be expected to identify and explain these concepts, and apply them to solve problems.
requirements: Read chapters 5 and 6 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen
Review class notes and examples on relations and functions
Complete practice problems on relations and functions before the quiz
The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions, short-answer questions, and problem-solving questions
Students are expected to bring a calculator and any other materials specified by the instructor

assignments.title: Relations and Functions Practice Problems
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will complete a set of practice problems focused on relations and functions in discrete mathematics. The goal is to reinforce understanding of these concepts through hands-on problem-solving.
requirements: Read sections 2.1 and 2.2 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen.
Complete problems 1-10 on page 55 of the textbook, which cover identifying relations and functions.
Solve exercises 1-5 on page 63, focusing on function operations and composition.
Provide detailed solutions to at least 5 additional problems of your choice from the end of section 2.2.
Submit your solutions in a clear and organized format, with each problem numbered and showing all work.

assignments.title: Graph Theory Quiz
assignments.description: The student will complete a quiz on Graph Theory, covering topics such as graph terminology, types of graphs, and basic graph algorithms. The quiz will assess the student's understanding of graph theory concepts, including graph representations, traversability, and connectivity.
requirements: The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions and short-answer problems.
The student must complete all questions within the given time limit.
The student must achieve a minimum score of 70% to pass the quiz.
The quiz will cover chapters 10-12 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen.

assignments.title: Graph Terminology and Types Checklist
assignments.description:
Complete a checklist of key graph terminology and types. Students should review the definitions and characteristics of various graph types, including simple graphs, multigraphs, pseudographs, directed graphs, and weighted graphs. The checklist should cover the following: 
  - Define and identify simple graphs, multigraphs, pseudographs, directed graphs, and weighted graphs.
  - List and describe the basic terminology used in graph theory, such as vertices, edges, adjacency, and degree of a vertex.
  - Identify and provide examples of different types of graphs and their applications.
  - Review and understand the concepts of graph isomorphism, graph connectivity, and graph traversability.
requirements: Complete the checklist of graph terminology and types provided in class.
Provide definitions and examples for each type of graph covered in the checklist.
Review and verify understanding of graph theory concepts through self-quizzing or peer discussion.
Submit the completed checklist as a PDF or Word document.

assignments.title: Combinatorics Quiz
assignments.description: The Combinatorics Quiz assesses your understanding of fundamental concepts in combinatorics, including permutations, combinations, and basic counting principles. You will be presented with multiple-choice questions that test your ability to apply these concepts to solve problems.
requirements: Read chapters 5 and 6 in the textbook 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen.
Review the lecture notes and examples provided for combinatorics.
Complete the practice problems on permutations and combinations.
Take the quiz within the allotted time frame (usually 60 minutes).
Answer all questions to the best of your ability.

assignments.title: Permutations and Combinations Practice Problems
assignments.description: For this written assignment, complete the practice problems related to permutations and combinations as outlined in chapters 5 and 6 of 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen. Be sure to show all work and explain your reasoning for each problem. The assignment should include solutions to at least 10 problems, with a focus on applying the concepts of permutations and combinations to solve real-world problems.
requirements: Complete all assigned practice problems from chapters 5 and 6 of the textbook
Show all work and explain your reasoning for each problem
Apply the concepts of permutations and combinations to solve real-world problems
Submit your work in a clear and legible format
Include a cover page with your name, assignment title, and week number

assignments.title: Propositional and Predicate Logic Quiz
assignments.description: The student will complete a quiz on propositional and predicate logic, demonstrating understanding of logical operators, truth tables, and proof techniques. The quiz will assess the student's ability to apply logical concepts to solve problems and evaluate arguments.
requirements: Read chapters 1 and 2 in the textbook, 'Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications' by Kenneth H. Rosen, focusing on propositional and predicate logic.
Review the lecture notes and examples provided in class, ensuring understanding of logical operators, truth tables, and proof techniques.
Complete practice problems from the textbook and in-class exercises to reinforce understanding of logical concepts.
The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions, short-answer questions, and problem-solving questions, covering topics such as logical operators, truth tables, and proof techniques.
The student must achieve a minimum score of 70% to pass the quiz.

assignments.title: Logical Operators and Proof Techniques Checklist
assignments.description: Complete a checklist of key logical operators and proof techniques in discrete mathematics. This checklist will cover the definitions and uses of various logical operators, including negation, conjunction, disjunction, implication, and equivalence. Additionally, it will review common proof techniques such as direct proof, proof by contrapositive, proof by contradiction, and proof by cases.
requirements: Define and provide examples of the following logical operators: negation, conjunction, disjunction, implication, and equivalence.
List and describe the steps for the following proof techniques: direct proof, proof by contrapositive, proof by contradiction, and proof by cases.
Provide examples of how to apply each logical operator and proof technique in a mathematical context.
Complete a series of exercises to practice identifying and applying logical operators and proof techniques.

assignments.title: Applications of Discrete Mathematics Project
assignments.description: For this project, you will apply the concepts of discrete mathematics to a real-world problem or scenario. Choose a topic that interests you, such as computer network design, cryptography, or optimization problems. Your task is to analyze the problem, identify the relevant discrete mathematics concepts, and propose a solution using those concepts. Your project should include a clear problem statement, a detailed analysis of the problem using discrete mathematics, and a well-supported solution.
requirements: The project should be 3-5 pages in length, not including references or appendices.
Include a clear and concise problem statement that sets the context for your project.
Identify and apply relevant discrete mathematics concepts, such as graph theory, combinatorics, or propositional and predicate logic.
Provide a detailed analysis of the problem and a well-supported solution.
Include a bibliography of at least 3 sources, properly cited.
Use proper mathematical notation and formatting throughout the project.

assignments.title: Review of Discrete Mathematics Concepts Quiz
assignments.description: The Review of Discrete Mathematics Concepts Quiz is a comprehensive assessment designed to evaluate students' understanding of key concepts covered throughout the course. This quiz will test students' knowledge of discrete mathematics topics, including set theory, relations, functions, graph theory, combinatorics, propositional and predicate logic, and mathematical proofs. Students are expected to review all previous course materials and come prepared to apply their knowledge to solve problems and answer questions.
requirements: Review all course materials from weeks 1-6.
Complete practice problems and critical thinking exercises provided in the course resources.
Take the quiz within the designated time frame, following the instructions provided.
Answer all questions to the best of your ability, showing all work and reasoning.

assignments.title: Practice Problems and Critical Thinking Exercises Checklist
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will complete a checklist of practice problems and critical thinking exercises. The goal is to reinforce understanding of discrete mathematics concepts covered throughout the course. Students should review all previous course material and complete the checklist by providing detailed solutions or explanations as required.
requirements: Review the course material from weeks 1-6
Complete the practice problems and critical thinking exercises as outlined in the checklist
Provide detailed solutions or explanations for each problem
Submit the completed checklist with all required parts filled out

assignments.title: Final Assessment
assignments.description: The final assessment is a comprehensive quiz that covers all the topics studied throughout the course. Students are expected to review and be familiar with the concepts and materials from weeks 1 to 7. The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions, short-answer questions, and problem-solving questions that assess students' understanding and application of discrete mathematics concepts.
requirements: Review all course materials and notes from weeks 1 to 7.
Complete a timed quiz consisting of 20 multiple-choice questions, 5 short-answer questions, and 2 problem-solving questions.
Submit the quiz within the given time frame (e.g., 2 hours).
Ensure all answers are clearly marked and submitted in the required format.
